description_long: This object is a pair of pliers with blue handles and metallic jaws, positioned open in a V-shape. The handles are robust and slightly contoured, featuring visible rivets and a matte finish, likely for improved grip. The jaws are metallic, tapering to a pointed tip, and appear to have a serrated inner surface for gripping objects securely. The pivot joint is prominent and circular, allowing the two arms to rotate smoothly around it. The pliers rest on a wooden plank, which serves as a base but is not part of the tool itself. The overall construction suggests durability and suitability for gripping, twisting, or cutting tasks in urban maintenance or repair scenarios.
description: A sturdy pair of blue-handled pliers with metallic, pointed, and serrated jaws, designed for gripping and manipulating objects.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open V-shape, blue handles, and pointed metallic jaws with visible serrations.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the thin profile of the pliers and the alignment of the handles and jaws.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, highlighting the handle contours and the back of the jaws.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim profile and the layered construction of the handles.
attribute_handle_type: Straight, slightly contoured, plastic-coated handles for ergonomic grip.
attribute_jaw_design: Pointed, serrated jaws with a narrow tip, suitable for precision gripping and cutting.
attribute_pivot_mechanism: Fixed single pivot joint, allowing standard opening and closing motion.
attribute_grip_pattern: Smooth plastic exterior with subtle contouring, no visible ribbing or knurling.
